review_findings.detail: nostr:npub1… pasted into the invite input failed post-S5 (parseContactCard branched only on a literal npub1 prefix while the old normaliseNpubPayload path stripped the nostr: scheme). Fixed at the single decode seam: parseContactCard now strips an optional case-insensitive nostr: prefix, which also repairs S4's Add-Contact paste path. Added a regression test.
onScan param named scannedNpub though it may now carry a card link/payload; renamed to scannedValue with a clarifying comment (matches AddContactModal).

AC-GRP-2 covered by a parity test (stub inviteByNpub mirroring the real KeyPackage rule) because no MarmotContext.inviteByNpub unit-test seam exists and MarmotContext is out of S5 scope. AC-GRP-2 judged MET; a real MarmotContext no_key_package unit test is a pre-existing gap → non-blocking backlog follow-up for the curator.

mocks.name: idb-keyval (Map-backed) in inviteByCard.test.ts
mocks.reason: InviteMemberModal.tsx imports useMarmot from MarmotContext, whose transitive dependency groupStorage.ts calls idb-keyval's createStore() at module load time. Reuses the exact no-op-store pattern already established by dmMessageEdits.test.ts / addContactCardWiring.test.ts (S4) — not a new pattern.

mocks.name: @/src/lib/ndkClient (getNdk/connectNdk) in inviteByCard.test.ts
mocks.reason: Same transitive-import concern as above; stubs the NDK entry points (reusing the S4 addContactCardWiring.test.ts pattern) so nothing in the import chain can attempt a real relay connection during the test run.

retrospective.harder_than_needed: AC-GRP-2's brief asked to verify the no_key_package failure mode 'using the real inviteByNpub path,' but the story's sole in-scope production file is InviteMemberModal.tsx and no pre-existing unit test exercises MarmotContext.inviteByNpub's KeyPackage-fetch branch at all (grep of app/tests/unit found nothing). MarmotContext.inviteByNpub is a useCallback closure inside the MarmotProvider component body, bound to refs (clientRef, groups, signerRef) populated by async init effects — there is no module-scope extraction of it comparable to fireAutoCommit, and adding one would mean editing MarmotContext.tsx, which is out of this story's file scope. Resolved by substituting a parity test: a stub inviteByNpub whose internal rule mirrors the real function's actual KeyPackage-presence check, invoked once with a bare npub and once with a card for the same pubkey, asserting identical call arguments and identical results. Documented as a deliberate, scope-respecting substitution in architecture.json's notes and in a new post-impl verification question (VQ-S5-009) rather than silently reinterpreting the AC.
retrospective.surprised_by: The InviteMemberModal.tsx production change ended up being additive-only and small enough (two exported functions, resolveInviteTarget/submitInvite, plus a three-line change to handleInvite's body) that it matched the S4 processContactInput extraction precedent almost exactly, including the same idb-keyval/ndkClient test-mocking shape — no design exploration was needed beyond confirming that precedent still applied here.
